step.actionDisplay: MOUSE(row=4, col=37) -> MOUSE(row=35, col=30) -> MOUSE(row=5, col=7) -> SPACE

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK so the strip is still purple top, white bottom even after I made the rectangle white and clicked on the white template part + SPACE. This means my hypothesis was wrong.
Let me reconsider. The strip shows:
- Top 5 rows: purple
- Bottom 5 rows: white
The template shows:
- Top 5 rows: white
- Bottom 5 rows: purple
So the strip is the INVERSE of the template. I clicked on the WHITE part of the template and SPACE resulted in purple top, white bottom.
Wait, maybe clicking on the white part of the template means "invert the pattern" or "use the opposite color". So clicking on white means "use purple" and clicking on purple means "use white"?
Or maybe the mechanism is different. Let me think about what I've observed:
1. Rectangle was purple, clicked white template, SPACE → strip became purple top, white bottom
2. Rectangle was white, clicked white template, SPACE → strip stayed purple top, white bottom
So the rectangle's color doesn't matter when clicking on the template. The strip pattern is determined by which part of the template I click.
Wait, but in case 1, the rectangle was purple and the strip became purple top. In case 2, the rectangle was white and the strip stayed purple top. So the rectangle's color DOESN'T determine the strip pattern when clicking on the template.
Actually, looking more carefully, in case 2 the strip didn't change at all (it was already purple top, white bottom from case 1). So clicking on the white template + SPACE always produces purple top, white bottom, regardless of the rectangle's color.
Hmm, so maybe clicking on the white part of the template ALWAYS produces the inverse pattern (purple top, white bottom), and clicking on the purple part of the template produces the matching pattern (white top, purple bottom)?
Let me test this: click on the PURPLE part of the template and SPACE.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click on the PURPLE part of the template and SPACE
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 10, 'col': 7}])
print(f"Click purple template: board_changed={result.get('board_changed', False)}")

result = action(['SPACE'])
print(f"SPACE: board_changed={result.get('board_changed', False)}, done={result.get('done', False)}")

# Check strip
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("Strip:")
for i in range(34, 44):
    print(f"  {i}: {ascii_lines[i][25:40]}")

# Check template for comparison
print("Template:")
for i in range(3, 13):
    line = ascii_lines[i]
    if any(c not in 'BcG' for c in line[0:15]):
        print(f"  {i}: {line[0:15]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
